articles.title: In China, swapping out an EV battery is as fast as filling up a gas tank
articles.summary: In China, some EV owners have the option to swap out a low battery for a fully charged one, instead of waiting at a charging station. The whole process only takes about three minutes.

articles.title: In Angola, Pope Leo XIV Faces the Legacy of Slavery
articles.summary: His visit includes a trip to a shrine where enslaved Africans were baptized before being forced into the treacherous voyage across the Atlantic Ocean.

articles.title: A Potent Threat in Strait of Hormuz: Iran’s “Mosquito Fleet”
articles.summary: Separate from the regular Iranian Navy, with boats that often go more than 115 miles per hour, it’s what a retired U.S. official calls a “disruptive force.”

articles.title: How Trump Helped Pope Leo Find His Voice
articles.summary: After his election last year, Leo XIV developed a reputation as a mild-mannered mediator. President Trump’s attacks have made him more combative.

articles.title: Federal Court Temporarily Freezes Nexstar’s Merger With Tegna
articles.summary: The judge said the two television companies could not combine operations while an antitrust lawsuit proceeded. Nexstar said its deal was already done.

articles.title: Trump Will Participate in a Marathon Bible Reading
articles.summary: He will read a passage from the Old Testament that his Christian supporters cite as a call to national repentance and divine blessing.

articles.title: Trump Extends Sanctions Exemption on Some Russian Oil as High Gas Prices Persist
articles.summary: The Trump administration made the announcement hours after Iran said that the Strait of Hormuz was open to commercial ships.

articles.title: Trump Is Urged to Move on Nuclear Site Thought to Be Beyond Reach of Bombs
articles.summary: Little is known about Pickaxe Mountain, but some experts say it illustrates the impossibility of relying on force alone to prevent Iran from acquiring a nuclear bomb.

articles.title: Judge halts local TV giant Nexstar's takeover of rival Tegna until trial
articles.summary: U.S. Judge Trevor Nunley ruled that consumers could suffer irreparable harm if Nexstar integrated Tegna's stations into its own operations ahead of an antitrust trial.

articles.title: Trump’s Dispute With Pope Leo Deepens Divisions on the Right
articles.summary: Sean Hannity criticized the pope. Tucker Carlson attacked Mr. Hannity. And President Trump suggested ranking MAGA figures: “good, bad, and somewhere in the middle.”

articles.title: Deaths of migrants in ICE custody hit record high under Trump
articles.summary: Twenty-nine people have died in ICE custody since October, the start of the federal government's fiscal year, already surpassing 2004's toll of 28, the previous record, according to government data.

articles.title: Animal activists celebrate their first global 'Sanctuary Day'
articles.summary: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.

articles.title: Hormuz reopens, but insurers aren't ready to sound the all-clear
articles.summary: Insurance for cargo and oil vessels stuck in the Strait of Hormuz has skyrocketed. How can insurers help us understand the realities of the war with Iran?

articles.title: Gasoline could drop below $4 in coming days
articles.summary: Gasoline costs should start to fall soon, although a full recovery to pre-war prices is expected to take months. That's assuming that peace holds and traffic flows resume through the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Why scientists are nervous about fungi
articles.summary: They can pose a threat to human health — yeast infections are but one example. Scientists say not enough attention is paid to their ability to develop resistance to medications that treat them.

articles.title: The Strait of Hormuz opens for commercial ships but the U.S. blockade continues on Iran
articles.summary: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.

articles.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon. And, Trump nominates a new CDC director.
articles.summary: A 10-day ceasefire to pause fighting between Israel and Hezb

articles.title: Who says they have no fear of the Trump administration? The quiz knows
articles.summary: Also: If you know what Eric Swalwell looks like, you'll get at least one question correct.

articles.title: Six months after ceasefire with Israel, people in Gaza say recovery hasn't even begun
articles.summary: Six months into a ceasefire that promised an end to the war and a surge of aid for Gaza, people say recovery hasn't even yet begun.

articles.title: UN looks for a short-term solution for moving fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The U.N. looks for a quick solution to get cargo ships with fertilizer through the Strait of Hormuz, to prevent a global food crisis.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
